title: tragacantha hostilis
intro: Tragacantha hostilis, also known as the stickseed or prickly stickseed, is a plant species in the milkwort family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides in its leaves and stems. The health benefits of Tragacantha hostilis include relief from minor wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues, making it a popular choice for topical and internal applications. Herbal preparations of Tragacantha hostilis can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
